summary: Migration MongoDB -> Supabase PostgreSQL validated end-to-end. 19/19 backend pytest cases pass (auth register/login/me, equipment public list & admin CRUD, loan lifecycle active/queued/return/cancel, duplicate-block, admin-only /loans/all, /stats, Supabase Storage /uploads/image upload + auth-required, AND the fairness algorithm in SQL: lower-history user activated ahead of an earlier-queued heavy user). Frontend e2e: cadastro -> auto-login (token saved) -> /equipamentos catalog (6 seeded items rendered) -> Solicitar agora modal -> Confirmar -> /painel shows ATIVOS=1 with NVIDIA RTX A6000 active and Devolver button. Stats card on landing already shows live PG data (6 equipamentos, users count from DB).

minor.issue: Schema has ON DELETE CASCADE for loans.equipment_id, so deleting an equipment with active loans silently wipes those loans. Better: refuse delete (409) or soft-delete when active loans exist.
CORS_ORIGINS='*' combined with allow_credentials=True is invalid per spec and will be rejected by browsers in production. Set explicit origins for prod.

INSERT->_recalc->_try_activate is wrapped in a transaction (good), but there's no row-level lock on equipment (FOR UPDATE) — under concurrent requests two queued rows could both attempt activation. For MVP traffic this is fine, but add SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on equipment row before reading available_units to be safe.
Pool min_size=1/max_size=10 with Transaction Pooler (statement_cache_size=0) is correct, but consider also setting timeout/lifetime for long-lived connections on Supabase pooler.

issues: Premium dark/orange theme is consistent, contrast looks fine, layout responsive on 1440x900. No issues observed.

action_items: (Optional/minor) Decide policy for deleting equipment with active loans (currently CASCADE wipes loans silently).
(Optional/minor) Add SELECT FOR UPDATE on equipment row inside the /loans/request transaction to prevent race under concurrent activation.
(Optional/prod) Replace CORS_ORIGINS='*' with explicit origins before production (incompatible with allow_credentials=True).
(Optional) Add data-testid attributes on key interactive elements (login/register inputs, Solicitar agora, Confirmar, Devolver, Cancelar, Admin add/delete) to harden future Playwright tests.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~712 lines mixing models, routes, helpers, schema DDL and seed. Works fine for MVP; for growth split into routers/, services/, schema/.
JWT_SECRET committed in backend/.env (preview only). Rotate before production.
Fairness algorithm logic is sound: ORDER BY fairness_score ASC, requested_at ASC verified empirically in TestFairness (lower-history user wins despite queueing later).
asyncpg pool created lazily and closed on shutdown — correct. statement_cache_size=0 properly set for Transaction Pooler (pgBouncer) on port 6543.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_fomerstick.py (added TestUploads class for Supabase Storage upload + auth check)

success_rate.frontend: 100% of tested flows (register -> request -> active loan in painel verified)

test_credentials: Admin (seeded in Postgres): admin@fomerstick.com / admin123. Test users created via /api/auth/register during tests.
seed_data_creation: Tests create ephemeral TEST_-prefixed equipment (cleaned up via DELETE) and disposable users (no delete endpoint exists; remain in DB).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend now on Supabase PostgreSQL via asyncpg Transaction Pooler (port 6543, statement_cache_size=0). DATABASE_URL in backend/.env. 6 equipment seeded on first startup. UUID PKs (gen_random_uuid). All datetime fields TIMESTAMPTZ converted to ISO via row_to_dict. Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_fomerstick.py covers auth, equipment, loan lifecycle, stats, Supabase Storage upload, and the smart-queue fairness in SQL. Frontend routes: /, /login, /cadastro, /equipamentos, /painel, /admin. Token stored at localStorage key 'fs_token'. Equipment list endpoint is public; loan endpoints require Bearer.
